items.question: Which task best plays to Claude's documented strengths?
options: Drafting and restructuring a technical design doc from bullet notes
Guaranteed correct real-time sports scores without tools
items.answer: Drafting and restructuring a technical design doc from bullet notes
items.explanation: Language-heavy synthesis and editing align with Claude's core design; physical or live-data tasks need other systems.

items.question: Why specify audience and format in prompts?
options: It increases steerability on tone and structure
It is required only on Haiku
items.answer: It increases steerability on tone and structure
items.explanation: Clear constraints help the model use its language strengths in the shape you need.

items.question: A strength of tiered models (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) is:
options: Every tier is identical speed and depth
You can trade cost and latency against capability per task
items.answer: You can trade cost and latency against capability per task
items.explanation: Model tiers let you route simple tasks to faster/cheaper models and hard tasks to more capable ones.

items.question: Why should you verify high-stakes outputs even when Claude performs well?
options: Models are probabilistic and can hallucinate or err on edge cases
Verification is only required on mobile apps
API calls skip the model entirely
items.answer: Models are probabilistic and can hallucinate or err on edge cases
items.explanation: Strengths describe tendencies, not guarantees—human or automated checks remain mandatory for critical work.
